methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $169M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $3.5B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Helion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series F), post-money $5.4B

data.narrative:
CarbonCure Technologies is a Series F climate and energy company that has raised $169M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (TAE Technologies at $6.0B Series G, Helion at $5.4B Series F, and Form Energy at $3.5B Series G), supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private frontier-tech comparables and 1 public peer.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 1.3x step-up multiplier for Series F advancement, implies $220M. Peer-median analysis across the three core comparables yields a median valuation of $5.4B, with a range of $3.5B to $6.0B. The wide dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity within the peer set—two comparables are Series G versus CarbonCure's Series F position.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $187M to $6.2B, with a median of $2.8B. The substantial gap between the stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects the early capital deployment against peer companies at later stages. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-equivalent discount for CarbonCure relative to Series G peers, and whether the peer set adequately reflects sector-specific risk and technology maturity differences within frontier energy.
